expectations.text: Applies actor framing — responsibility to actors, not method count
expectations.evidence: 'A class should be responsible to one actor (one group of stakeholders).' and 'reason to change is a human/business axis, not a syntactic one'

expectations.text: Notes all four methods serve one actor
expectations.evidence: 'There is one actor here: the auth/identity team.'

expectations.text: Names this as speculative / premature SRP
expectations.evidence: Uses 'SRP-theater' and 'needless decomposition driven by a misreading of SRP' — captures the idea but does not name 'speculative SRP' as the anti-pattern

expectations.text: Right trigger: real change request from different stakeholder
expectations.evidence: 'When the split WOULD be right' section lists 'Different actors' as the first signal
